teaching_contract.concrete_problem: A model completion cannot be debugged or trusted when the harness loses the exact messages sent, returned text, and provider evidence.
teaching_contract.first_principle: The model returns data; the harness owns the boundary that records and normalizes that data before the rest of the system uses it.
worked_walkthrough: Follow one FakeModel call from the supplied message list to a provider response.
Trace how call_model turns the response into one ModelCallRecord instead of exposing provider-specific data throughout the system.

boundary_and_invariant.invariant: Every successful call preserves the exact input messages, returns assistant text as a string, and retains inspectable evidence.

options: Coerce arbitrary provider output into text so the loop continues.
Reject a non-string completion at the provider boundary.
design_tension.decision_rule: Reject data that violates the internal text contract; preserving an explicit failure is safer than hiding a provider-shape change.

teaching_contract.prediction_prompt: Before opening the implementation, predict what call_model should do when FakeModel returns a non-string completion and explain why.
teaching_contract.artifact_inspection_prompt: Inspect the starter types and failing FakeModel test; identify which facts exist before call_model is implemented and which record fields the test can prove.

build: Implement the narrow call_model normalization and ModelCallRecord creation path.
leave_unchanged: Do not add tool dispatch, retries, or message-state policy to this primitive.

proof_interpretation.establishes: The focused tests establish the input, output-type, and evidence-record contract for the fake provider cases.
proof_interpretation.does_not_establish: They do not establish that a real provider is reliable, that tools execute safely, or that a multi-step agent can recover.

teaching_contract.transfer_prompt: If a second provider returns a rich response object, decide whether its metadata belongs in the record, the normalized text contract, or a provider adapter.

0001-model-call-primitive.learning_goal: Preserve an inspectable model-call record while normalizing provider output at one boundary.
0001-model-call-primitive.learner_decision: Decide which provider response data belongs in ModelCallRecord and reject a non-string completion.

target_artifacts.expected_artifact: A ModelCallRecord with exact messages, string output, and evidence fields.

failure_contract.symptom: A provider response contains a non-string completion.

micro_world.rationale: One annotated request and response exposes the complete boundary.

teaching_contract.concrete_problem: An agent repeats read_file even after the tool succeeded because the next model call cannot see the observation.
teaching_contract.first_principle: An agent has no hidden durable memory between calls; its message sequence is the state handed back to the model.
worked_walkthrough: Compare the first model-call input with the second after read_file returns a result.
Follow the user task, assistant request, and tool observation in order to see why the second call can make a different decision.

boundary_and_invariant.boundary: Message-state accumulation owns the transition from an event to a correctly ordered next-call message.
boundary_and_invariant.invariant: Before a next model call, every relevant tool observation is appended after the request that caused it without changing prior message order.

options: Ask the model again and rely on it to remember the tool result.
Append a normalized tool observation to explicit message state before asking again.
design_tension.decision_rule: Make evidence explicit in the next input; the harness, not an assumed model memory, owns the state transition.

teaching_contract.prediction_prompt: Given two calls and one successful read_file result, predict what must be present in the second call's messages for repetition to be diagnosable as missing context.
teaching_contract.artifact_inspection_prompt: Inspect ModelCallRecord from Primitive 1 and the message-state starter test to separate recorded provider evidence from the new state that must be accumulated.

build: Implement ordered conversion and appending of user, assistant, and tool messages.
leave_unchanged: Do not call the model again, reorder history, or decide whether a tool request is valid.

proof_interpretation.establishes: The second-call proof establishes that the model receives the user, assistant, and tool observation in the required order.
proof_interpretation.does_not_establish: It does not prove that every model will stop repeating a request or that the requested tool was safe to run.

teaching_contract.transfer_prompt: When a tool fails instead of succeeding, decide which observation should still be appended and why hiding that failure would make the next decision worse.

0002-message-state-primitive.learning_goal: Preserve ordered messages so a tool observation reaches the next model call.
0002-message-state-primitive.learner_decision: Diagnose whether a repeated tool request is model behavior or missing message-state context.

target_artifacts.expected_artifact: A second model call that includes the prior tool observation.

failure_contract.symptom: The agent repeats read_file after it already succeeded.

micro_world.rationale: A short timeline helps expose accumulation, but a full simulation is unnecessary.

teaching_contract.concrete_problem: Assistant output may look like a tool request but be malformed JSON, a JSON value of the wrong outer shape, or trailing prose.
teaching_contract.first_principle: Text becomes structured data only at an explicit parser boundary; parsing proves syntax and outer shape, not executability.
worked_walkthrough: Follow a valid JSON object from raw assistant text into ToolRequest.
Contrast it with a JSON string and trailing prose to locate the first claim the parser can no longer make.

boundary_and_invariant.boundary: The tool-request parser owns JSON decoding and ToolRequest outer-shape checks.
boundary_and_invariant.invariant: It returns either one structured ToolRequest or a parse/outer-shape error without deciding tool existence or argument validity.

options: Accept convenient function-like or prose-wrapped syntax from the model.
Accept only one complete JSON object with the required outer shape.
design_tension.decision_rule: Keep the protocol strict and the parser's guarantee small so later boundaries receive unambiguous structured data.

teaching_contract.prediction_prompt: Classify each supplied assistant output as parser-acceptable or parser-rejectable before reading the parser implementation, and state the first failed condition.
teaching_contract.artifact_inspection_prompt: Inspect the previous message-state output shape and the parser test inputs; identify the precise point where raw text crosses into ToolRequest.

build: Implement the JSON and outer-shape parser contract plus its focused cases.
leave_unchanged: Do not add a tool registry, argument schemas, or runtime handler lookup.

proof_interpretation.establishes: The parser tests establish behavior for the configured raw-text cases, including valid requests and invalid trailing prose.
proof_interpretation.does_not_establish: They do not prove that a parsed tool exists, that its arguments are safe, or that a handler can execute it.

teaching_contract.transfer_prompt: For a valid JSON array containing one tool-like object, decide whether the parser should accept it and which later lesson, if any, should own multi-action support.

0003-manual-tool-protocol.learning_goal: Distinguish valid tool-request JSON from malformed or unsupported model output.
0003-manual-tool-protocol.learner_decision: Locate the parser boundary without assigning schema or registry failures to it.

target_artifacts.expected_artifact: A structured ToolRequest or a parse/outer-shape error, without tool-registry or argument validation.

failure_contract.symptom: Assistant output is malformed JSON or not a ToolRequest-shaped object.

micro_world.rationale: Immediate input and output feedback is useful, but persistent world state is not.

teaching_contract.concrete_problem: ToolRequest(tool=read_file, args={path: 123}) is parsed correctly but is not safe or meaningful to execute.
teaching_contract.first_principle: A parsed request is only structured text; a validation boundary must establish the tool-specific contract before runtime execution.
worked_walkthrough: Start with Primitive 3's ToolRequest to identify exactly what parsing already guarantees.
Walk the path type failure through registry lookup and argument validation before a runtime handler is even considered.

boundary_and_invariant.boundary: Tool argument validation owns protocol registry lookup and the registered tool's argument contract.
boundary_and_invariant.invariant: Only a request for a known tool with arguments matching its declared schema can leave validation as executable input.

options: Let read_file discover malformed arguments during execution.
Reject malformed or unknown requests before runtime dispatch using the protocol contract.
design_tension.decision_rule: Validate at the trust boundary, while keeping runtime-handler availability a distinct later decision rather than pretending one registry answers both questions.

teaching_contract.prediction_prompt: Before opening the validator, decide which boundary owns path=123, where known tools are declared, and whether runtime handler absence is the same failure.
teaching_contract.artifact_inspection_prompt: Inspect ToolRequest and the parser cases from Primitive 3; state what those artifacts prove and the new responsibility they deliberately leave open.

build: Implement known-tool and argument-shape validation with explicit result codes.
leave_unchanged: Do not execute read_file or merge protocol registry ownership with runtime handler availability.

proof_interpretation.establishes: The validation cases establish the declared protocol's behavior for known tools and their arguments.
proof_interpretation.does_not_establish: They do not establish filesystem permission, handler registration, or successful tool execution.

teaching_contract.transfer_prompt: For submit, decide whether protocol-valid but non-executable control should pass validation, fail dispatch, or terminate before dispatch, and name the evidence needed to decide.

0004-schema-validation.learning_goal: Validate registered tool arguments at the trust boundary before execution.
0004-schema-validation.learner_decision: Consume Primitive 3's ToolRequest and assign protocol registry, argument schema, and runtime-handler availability to their responsible source of truth.

target_artifacts.expected_artifact: Validation that accepts a Primitive 3 ToolRequest only when its tool and arguments are executable by the protocol contract.

failure_contract.symptom: A parsed read_file request has path=123.

micro_world.rationale: Validation outcomes are immediate and do not require an evolving simulated world.

teaching_contract.concrete_problem: A protocol-valid read_file request can still traverse outside the configured sandbox or produce a confusing file-system result.
teaching_contract.first_principle: Validation of an argument's shape is not authorization of the resource it names; the capability boundary must enforce containment at use time.
worked_walkthrough: Follow a relative nested path and a parent-traversal path through sandbox resolution.
Compare FORBIDDEN_PATH with missing-file and directory results to see why one generic failure would destroy useful causal evidence.

boundary_and_invariant.boundary: The sandboxed file-tool boundary owns canonical path containment and file-operation result normalization.
boundary_and_invariant.invariant: No read or write resolves outside the configured root, while allowed paths retain accurate success or failure observations.

options: Trust validated relative-looking paths and pass them directly to filesystem operations.
Resolve paths against the sandbox and reject any result that escapes it.
design_tension.decision_rule: Authorize the resolved capability, not a string that merely appears harmless before normalization.

teaching_contract.prediction_prompt: For each path case, predict whether it is contained, which result code applies, and why argument validation alone cannot decide it.
teaching_contract.artifact_inspection_prompt: Inspect Primitive 4's accepted request shape and the real file-tool cases; distinguish a protocol-valid request from an authorized filesystem operation.

build: Implement or extend contained read/write behavior and add one regression case tied to a rejected path.
leave_unchanged: Do not change parser syntax rules or redefine the protocol registry while fixing containment.

proof_interpretation.establishes: The focused file-tool proof establishes containment and normalized outcomes for its covered path and operation cases.
proof_interpretation.does_not_establish: It does not prove that every filesystem race, symlink policy, or future tool capability is safe.

teaching_contract.transfer_prompt: If a future tool accepts a URL rather than a path, decide which part of the containment reasoning transfers and which new authority boundary it needs.

0005-sandboxed-file-tools.learning_goal: Enforce filesystem containment while returning useful file-tool observations.
0005-sandboxed-file-tools.learner_decision: Decide which path failures the tool boundary must reject and why validation alone is insufficient.

target_artifacts.expected_artifact: File tools that reject forbidden paths with FORBIDDEN_PATH.

failure_contract.symptom: A request traverses out of the configured sandbox.

micro_world.rationale: Spatial containment can benefit from manipulation, but existing path cases may teach it sufficiently.

teaching_contract.system_problem: A protocol-valid read_file request can reach the agent loop while the runtime tools dictionary has no read_file handler; the harness still has to preserve a bounded, explainable trajectory.
teaching_contract.integration_responsibility: The loop coordinates already-built boundaries in a defined order, turns recoverable failures into observations, maintains message state, and stops safely.

existing_components.already_guarantees: Raw assistant text becomes either a structured request or a normalized parse failure.
existing_components.does_not_guarantee: The requested tool exists or can execute.

existing_components.already_guarantees: A known protocol tool has arguments that match its registered schema.
existing_components.does_not_guarantee: The runtime tools dictionary has a handler for that tool.

existing_components.already_guarantees: A permitted file operation returns a normalized result while containing paths.
existing_components.does_not_guarantee: The loop invoked it in the right order or will stop safely.

components.output_or_transition: Validated request or normalized rejection observation
components.responsibility: Establish the protocol contract before runtime dispatch.

components.input: Validated non-submit request and runtime handler map
components.output_or_transition: Tool result, missing-handler observation, or normalized tool exception
components.responsibility: Decide whether an executable handler is actually available.

components.input: User task, messages, model output, and each boundary result
components.output_or_transition: Next ordered messages or a submitted/max-steps AgentResult
components.responsibility: Own transition order, observation accumulation, and bounded stopping.

system_model.system_invariant: Every loop iteration records assistant output, invokes boundaries in order, appends a normalized observation for non-terminal outcomes, and never exceeds max_steps.

success_path: The model requests read_file with valid JSON and arguments.
The registered runtime handler returns a result that is appended as a tool observation before the next call, which submits the final answer.
failure_or_edge_path: The protocol registry accepts read_file, but runtime dispatch finds no read_file handler.
The loop appends UNKNOWN_TOOL as an observation, gives the model another bounded turn, and eventually returns submit or max_steps.
worked_trajectories.comparison_question: What evidence distinguishes an unknown protocol tool from a protocol-valid tool whose runtime handler is missing?

options: Abort the entire run whenever any component rejects a request.
Normalize recoverable parser, validation, and dispatch failures into observations while enforcing a bounded step budget.
design_tension.decision_rule: The loop preserves recoverable evidence for the next model decision, but it owns explicit terminal control flow for submit and max_steps.

teaching_contract.prediction_prompt: Before opening run_agent, predict the next message and responsible boundary when read_file validates successfully but the runtime tools dictionary is empty.
teaching_contract.artifact_inspection_prompt: Inspect the parser, validator, file-tool contracts, agent-loop tests, and causal scenario to map what each component proves before the loop composes them.

intervention_strategy.scaffold: The real agent_loop.py reconstruction handoff, with the control-flow slice intentionally withheld from copying.
build_order: Append assistant output to message state.
Validate the output and append a normalized rejection observation when validation fails.
Return immediately for submit control flow.
Look up and invoke a runtime handler only after validation.
Normalize missing handlers and tool exceptions into observations.
Stop after the configured max_steps budget.
learner_owns: The order of loop transitions and message-state updates.
At least one normalized runtime failure path and its regression evidence.
leave_unchanged: Parser, validator, and file-tool internals unless diagnosis identifies them as the first bad boundary.
forbidden_shortcuts: Treating a tool exception as invisible control flow.

required_evidence: A normalized missing-runtime-handler or tool-exception trajectory.
integration_proof.establishes: The focused suite establishes the tested ordering, failure normalization, dispatch, submit, and bounded-stop behavior of this composed harness.
integration_proof.does_not_establish: It does not establish reliable model behavior, correctness of every future tool, production observability, or broad behavioral evaluation coverage.

teaching_contract.causal_explanation_prompt: Explain one real failed trajectory as component transitions and contracts; do not attribute the failure merely to the model behaving strangely.
teaching_contract.transfer_prompt: Change one condition—malformed output, invalid arguments, a missing runtime handler, a tool exception, or exhausted budget—and name the first bad transition, smallest safe intervention, and regression proof.

0006-agent-loop-primitive.learning_goal: Predict and explain how the harness advances, rejects, executes, recovers, and stops.
0006-agent-loop-primitive.learner_decision: Diagnose the first bad transition and choose the smallest safe correction across parser, validator, runtime registry, and loop control.

target_artifacts.expected_artifact: A bounded loop that normalizes failures and preserves message-state observations.

failure_contract.symptom: A protocol-valid tool can be absent from the runtime handlers.

micro_world.rationale: Harness decisions evolve through hidden, branching state and are best learned causally.

0007-trace-logger.learning_goal: Use a causal trace to separate model behavior from harness behavior.
0007-trace-logger.learner_decision: Design and implement the smallest causal trace that distinguishes model output, harness decisions, tool outcomes, and stop reason.

target_artifacts.expected_artifact: A replayable JSON trace with one causal record for every agent-loop decision.

failure_contract.symptom: A final answer without causal fields cannot diagnose the run.

micro_world.rationale: A real incomplete run makes the missing causal evidence directly visible.

0008-eval-runner.learning_goal: Evaluate multiple trajectories against explicit behavioral assertions.
0008-eval-runner.learner_decision: Implement a report that grades composed cases and preserves causal evidence for each failure.

target_artifacts.expected_artifact: A per-case eval report with expected and actual behavior, failure reason, and trace path.

failure_contract.symptom: Focused tests do not establish a composed user workflow.

micro_world.rationale: A real composed workflow fixture makes assertion coverage and aggregate outcomes concrete.
